candidate.description: Comparing replica datasets via hierarchical hash trees so that only differing subranges are exchanged, dramatically reducing bandwidth for anti-entropy repair.

candidates_full.full_description: Background process reconciling divergent replicas in eventually consistent systems; Merkle-tree-based diffing is one specific implementation but not previously catalogued as an L2.
Repairing replica divergence on the read path; complementary to background anti-entropy but distinct mechanism.
